public_functions.behavior_description: Enable the GTM module and its functional clocks needed for TOM operation. Initialize a unified PWM configuration with defaults, then set: center-aligned mode, complementary output pairing, active-high polarity for both sides, push-pull output mode, and the requested pad driver. Select a TOM submodule with one base (time) channel as the time base and three complementary channel pairs as synchronized channels. Assign the six output pins for the complementary pairs using the generic GTM pin-mapping API. Initialize the PWM driver with the channel array and configuration, program the target PWM frequency (20 kHz) immediately, and configure a uniform hardware dead-time across all complementary pairs. Prepare an initial duty vector for the three phases (25%, 50%, 75%) and apply it in one synchronous update so all channels take effect together. Finally, start the synchronized channels and enable channel outputs so the PWM waveforms are driven on the configured pins.

On each call, compute a duty increment as a fixed fraction of the PWM period (duty step). For each of the three phase duties, add the step and constrain the result using lower and upper bounds (minimum and maximum duty limits). If a channel’s duty reaches or exceeds the maximum bound, wrap it to the minimum bound. After updating all three duty values, build a duty array ordered consistently with the configured PWM channels and perform a single synchronous duty update so all complementary outputs change coherently while maintaining complementary behavior and the configured dead-time.
